question: Who said, "How shall I curse, whom God hath not cursed?" when asked to prophesy against Israel?

question: Who said, "Whereas I was blind, now I see"?

A leper who returned to thank Jesus

question: Who said, "I know that my redeemer lives"?
verseText: For I know that my Redeemer lives.

question: Finish the verse: "Be still, and know that I am ____."
verseText: Be still, and know that I am God.

question: Finish the verse: "Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of ____."
verseText: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.

question: Finish the verse: "Do not be anxious about anything, but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to ____."
verseText: Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God.

question: Finish the verse: "Commit thy works unto the Lord, and thy ____ shall be established."
verseText: Commit your works to the LORD, and your thoughts shall be established.

question: Finish the verse: "And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his ____."
verseText: And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.

question: Finish the verse: "A soft answer turneth away ____: but grievous words stir up anger."
verseText: A soft answer turns away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger.

question: Finish the verse: "Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not ____."
verseText: Charity suffers long, and is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up.

question: Finish the verse: "I will lift up mine eyes unto the hills, from whence cometh my ____."
verseText: I will lift up mine eyes to the hills, from whence comes my help.

question: Finish the verse: "The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more ____."
verseText: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have it more abundantly.

question: Finish the verse: "Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart ____."
verseText: Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.

question: Finish the verse: "No man can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and ____."
verseText: You cannot serve God and mammon.

question: Finish the verse: "For the Lord God is a sun and shield: the Lord will give grace and glory: no good thing will he withhold from them that walk ____."
verseText: No good thing will he withhold from them that walk uprightly.

question: Finish the verse: "Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due ____."
verseText: Humble yourselves therefore under the mighty hand of God, that he may exalt you in due time.

question: Finish the verse: "Ho, every one that thirsteth, come ye to the waters; and he that hath no money; come ye, buy, and eat; yea, come, buy wine and milk without ____ and without price."
verseText: Ho, every one that thirsteth, come you to the waters... buy wine and milk without money and without price.

question: Finish the verse: "What therefore God hath joined together, let not ____ put asunder."
verseText: What therefore God has joined together, let not man put asunder.

question: Finish the verse: "The heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately ____: who can know it?"
verseText: The heart is deceitful above all things, and desperately wicked: who can know it?

question: Finish the verse: "For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound ____."
verseText: For God has not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.

question: Finish the verse: "Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in ____."
verseText: Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven.

question: Finish the verse: "As far as the east is from the west, so far hath he removed our ____ from us."
verseText: As far as the east is from the w, so far has he removed our transgressions from us.

question: Finish the verse: "For by grace are ye saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the ____ of God."
verseText: For by grace are you saved through faith; and that not of yourselves: it is the gift of God.

question: Finish the verse: "Praise ye the Lord. Praise God in his sanctuary: praise him in the firmament of his ____."
verseText: Praise you the Lord. Praise God in his sanctuary: praise him in the firmament of his power.

question: Finish the verse: "For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ: for it is the power of God unto salvation to every one that ____."
verseText: For I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ: for it is the power of God to salvation to every one that believes.

question: Finish the verse: "The Lord is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart; and saveth such as be of a contrite ____."
verseText: The Lord is nigh to them that are of a broken heart; and saves such as be of a contrite spirit.

question: Finish the verse: "Likewise the Spirit also helpeth our infirmities: for we know not what we should pray for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh ____ for us with groanings which cannot be uttered."
verseText: The Spirit itself makes intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered.

question: Finish the verse: "A merry heart doeth good like a ____: but a broken spirit drieth the bones."
verseText: A merry heart doeth good like a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones.

question: Finish the verse: "I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do ____."
verseText: I am the vine, you are the branches... for without me you can do nothing.

question: Finish the verse: "My son, if sinners entice thee, ____ thou not."
verseText: My son, if sinners entice you, consent you not.

question: Finish the verse: "Whosoever shall confess that Jesus is the Son of God, God dwelleth in him, and he in ____."
verseText: Whoever shall confess that Jesus is the Son of God, God dwells in him, and he in God.

question: Finish the verse: "Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceeds out of the mouth of ____."
verseText: Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceeds from the mouth of God.

question: Finish the verse: "The just shall live by ____."
verseText: The just shall live by his faith.

question: Finish the verse: "Be ye doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving your own ____."
verseText: Be doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving yourselves.

question: Finish the verse: "All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in ____."
verseText: All scripture is given by inspiration of God... for instruction in righteousness.

question: Finish the verse: "Not by might, nor by power, but by my Spirit, saith the Lord of ____."
verseText: Not by might, nor by power, but by my spirit, says the LORD of hosts.

question: Finish the verse: "Faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of ____."
verseText: So then faith comes by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.

question: Finish the verse: "For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come, nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus ____."
verseText: Nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

question: Finish the verse: "Surely he hath borne our griefs, and carried our sorrows: yet we did esteem him stricken, smitten of God, and ____."
verseText: Surely he has borne our griefs, and carried our sorrows: yet we did esteem him stricken, smitten of God, and afflicted.

question: Finish the verse: "For the word of God is quick, and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged sword, piercing even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the ____."
verseText: For the word of God is quick, and powerful... and is a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.

question: Finish the verse: "For my thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways my ways, saith the Lord. For as the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways, and my ____ than your thoughts."
verseText: For my thoughts are not your thoughts... so are my ways higher than your ways, and my thoughts than your thoughts.

question: Finish the verse: "Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things ____."
verseText: Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.

question: Finish the verse: "He is despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows, and acquainted with ____."
verseText: He is despised and rejected of men; a man of sorrows, and acquainted with grief.

question: Finish the verse: "But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, ____."
verseText: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, temperance.

question: Finish the verse: "For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given: and the government shall be upon his shoulder: and his name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, The mighty God, The everlasting Father, The Prince of ____."
verseText: And his name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, The mighty God, The everlasting Father, The Prince of Peace.

question: Finish the verse: "Wherefore take unto you the whole armour of God, that ye may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to ____."
verseText: Therefore take to you the whole armour of God, that you may be able to withstand in the evil day, and having done all, to stand.

question: Finish the verse: "For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, saith the Lord, thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected ____."
verseText: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you, says the LORD, thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected end.

question: Finish the verse: "For the preaching of the cross is to them that perish foolishness; but unto us which are saved it is the ____ of God."
verseText: For the preaching of the cross is to them that perish foolishness; but to us which are saved it is the power of God.

question: Finish the verse: "Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly, nor standeth in the way of sinners, nor sitteth in the seat of the ____."
verseText: Blessed is the man that walks not in the counsel of the ungodly... nor sits in the seat of the scornful.

question: Finish the verse: "Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new ____: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new."
verseText: Therefore if any man be in Christ, he is a new creature: old things are passed away; behold, all things are become new.

question: Finish the verse: "He that dwelleth in the secret place of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the ____."
verseText: He that dwells in the secret place of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.

question: Finish the verse: "Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for ____."
verseText: Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.

question: Finish the verse: "Come now, and let us reason together, saith the Lord: though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be as white as ____; though they be red like crimson, they shall be as wool."
verseText: Though your sins be as scarlet, they shall be as white as snow; though they be red like crimson, they shall be as wool.

question: Finish the verse: "And he said to them all, If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his ____, and follow me."
verseText: If any man will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross daily, and follow me.

question: Finish the verse: "Finally, brethren, whatsoever things are true, whatsoever things are honest, whatsoever things are just, whatsoever things are pure, whatsoever things are lovely, whatsoever things are of good report... think on these ____."
verseText: Think on these things.

question: Finish the verse: "But they that wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles; they shall run, and not be weary; and they shall walk, and not ____."
verseText: They shall run, and not be weary; and they shall walk, and not faint.

question: Finish the verse: "But without faith it is impossible to please him: for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a rewarder of them that diligently ____ him."
verseText: He is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him.

question: Finish the verse: "The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want. He maketh me to lie down in green pastures: he leadeth me beside the still ____."
verseText: He makes me to lie down in green pastures: he leads me beside the still waters.

question: Finish the verse: "That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised him from the dead, thou shalt be ____."
verseText: That if you shall confess with your mouth the Lord Jesus, and shall believe in your heart that God has raised him from the dead, you shall be saved.

question: Finish the verse: "Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above ____."
verseText: Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price is far above rubies.

question: Finish the verse: "There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who walk not after the flesh, but after the ____."
verseText: There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Spirit.

question: Finish the verse: "Even the youths shall faint and be weary, and the young men shall utterly fall: but they that wait upon the Lord shall ____ their strength."
verseText: Even the youths shall faint and be weary... but those who wait on the LORD shall renew their strength.

question: Finish the verse: "For the love of money is the root of all ____: which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith."
verseText: For the love of money is the root of all evil: which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith.

question: Finish the verse: "For the Lord knoweth the way of the righteous: but the way of the ungodly shall ____."
verseText: For the Lord knows the way of the righteous: but the way of the ungodly shall perish.

question: Which prophet was swallowed by a great fish?
verseText: Now the LORD had prepared a great fish to swallow Jonah.

question: Which prophet interpreted dreams in Babylon?
verseText: The secret was revealed to Daniel in a night vision.

question: Who confronted the prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel?
verseText: I alone am left a prophet of the LORD; but Baal's prophets are four hundred and fifty men.

question: Which prophet confronted King David after his sin with Bathsheba?
verseText: The LORD sent Nathan to David.

question: Which prophet was taken up to heaven in a whirlwind?
verseText: Elijah went up by a whirlwind into heaven.

question: Choose One: Isaiah prophesied that a virgin would conceive and bear a son.
verseText: Behold, the virgin shall conceive and bear a Son.

question: Which prophet said, 'Who is a God like unto thee, that pardoneth iniquity'?
verseText: Who is a God like You, pardoning iniquity...?

question: Which prophet was called while he was a youth, saying he could not speak?
verseText: Ah, Lord GOD! Behold, I cannot speak, for I am a youth.

question: What city did God send Jonah to preach to?
verseText: Arise, go to Nineveh, that great city, and cry out against it.

question: What did Isaiah see in his vision that said 'Holy, holy, holy is the Lord of hosts'?
choices: Angels with six wings called seraphim
verseText: Above it stood seraphim... And one cried to another and said: 'Holy, holy, holy is the LORD of hosts.'

question: Which prophet predicted there would be a new covenant written on the heart?
verseText: I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel... I will put my law in their inward parts, and write it in their hearts.

question: Which prophet announced 'The spirit of the Lord God is upon me; because the Lord hath anointed me to preach good tidings'?
verseText: The spirit of the LORD God is upon me; because the Lord has anointed me to preach good tidings to the meek.

question: Who was the prophet that poured water on the hands of Elijah and later succeeded him?
verseText: Elisha the son of Shaphat... shall you anoint to be prophet in your room.

question: What did God touch to purify the prophet Isaiah for his mission?
choices: A glowing coal from the altar
verseText: Then flew one of the seraphim to me, having a live coal in his hand... and he laid it upon my mouth, and said, Lo, this has touched your lips; and your iniquity is taken away.

question: Which prophet said 'Seek the Lord while he may be found; call ye upon him while he is near'?
verseText: Seek you the Lord while he may be found, call you upon him while he is near.

question: According to Malachi, what was Israel robbing God of?
verseText: Wherein have we robbed you? In tithes and offerings.

question: Which prophet declared 'I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and daughters shall prophesy'?
verseText: I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh; and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy.

question: What did Jonah do when God relented and did not destroy Nineveh?
choices: He returned to Israel and praised God
verseText: But it displeased Jonah exceedingly, and he was very angry.

question: Which prophet warned 'Can two walk together, except they be agreed'?
verseText: Can two walk together, except they be agreed?

question: Which Old Testament prophet is quoted most frequently in the New Testament?
verseText: Isaiah is the most frequently quoted Old Testament prophet in the New Testament.

question: Elijah was fed by ravens at what brook during the drought?
verseText: Get you hence, and turn you eastward, and hide thyself by the brook Cherith... I have commanded the ravens to feed you there.

question: Which prophet said 'He hath shewed thee, O man, what is good; and what doth the Lord require of thee, but to do justly, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with thy God'?
verseText: What does the Lord require of you, but to do justly, and to love mercy, and to walk humbly with your God?

question: What was Elijah's prayer that ended the three-year drought in Israel?
choices: He prayed on his knees with his hands raised
He prayed seven times with his face between his knees
He prayed at midnight under the stars
verseText: Elijah went up to the top of Carmel; and he cast himself down upon the earth, and put his face between his knees.

question: Which prophet said the Lord's mercies are new every morning and great is his faithfulness?
verseText: It is of the LORD's mercies that we are not consumed... they are new every morning: great is your faithfulness.

question: What sign did God give Hezekiah through Isaiah to confirm he would be healed?
choices: A dove landed on the temple
The shadow on the sundial went backward ten degrees
A star appeared in the east
Rain fell after a long drought
verseText: The shadow returned ten degrees, by which degrees it was gone down.

question: Which prophet called out 'Here am I; send me' when God asked who would go for them?
verseText: Also I heard the voice of the LORD, saying, Whom shall I send...? Then said I, Here am I; send me.

question: In what city did Daniel serve as a royal advisor?
verseText: Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came to Jerusalem... Daniel was among them.

question: Which prophet said 'They that wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles'?
verseText: Those who wait on the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings like eagles.

question: Which prophet was thrown into a muddy pit for his unpopular message?
verseText: Then took they Jeremiah, and cast him into the dungeon.

question: Which prophet was a shepherd and dresser of sycamore trees before God called him?
verseText: The words of Amos, who was among the herdmen of Tekoa.

question: Which prophet warned Israel, 'Prepare to meet thy God'?
verseText: Prepare to meet your God, O Israel.

question: What happened to Daniel when he was thrown into the lions' den?
choices: The lions did not harm him
verseText: My God has sent his angel, and has shut the lions' mouths, that they have not hurt me.

question: Malachi promised God would open the windows of heaven for blessings if Israel did what?
choices: Brought all the tithes into the storehouse
Kept the feasts of the Lord
verseText: Bring you all the tithes into the storehouse... and I will... open you the windows of heaven.

question: Which prophet is often called the 'weeping prophet' because of his grief over Israel's sin?
verseText: Oh that my head were waters, and mine eyes a fountain of tears, that I might weep day and night for the slain of the daughter of my people!

question: Which prophet foretold the Messiah would be born in Bethlehem?
verseText: Out of you shall come forth to Me the One to be Ruler in Israel.

question: Which prophet saw a valley of dry bones?
verseText: He set me down in the midst of the valley; and it was full of bones.

question: Which prophet declared, 'The just shall live by faith'?
verseText: The just shall live by his faith.

question: Which prophet married Gomer as a sign to Israel?
verseText: Go, take to you a wife of whoredoms... So he went and took Gomer.

question: Which prophet saw the Lord high and lifted up in the temple?
verseText: I saw also the Lord sitting upon a throne, high and lifted up.

question: Which prophet foretold that the righteous king would come humble and riding upon a donkey?
verseText: Behold, your King comes to you... lowly, and riding upon an ass.

question: Who am I? I was told to take off my sandals on holy ground, then sent to deliver a message to Pharaoh.
verseText: Put off your shoes from off your feet... I will send you to Pharaoh.

question: Isaiah 53 describes a suffering servant. What does it say about his appearance?
choices: He was glorious and beautiful to see
He had no form or comeliness and no beauty that we should desire him
He was tall and strong like a cedar
He was clothed in white linen
verseText: He has no form nor comeliness; and when we shall see him, there is no beauty that we should desire him.

question: What did God tell Jeremiah before he was born?
choices: He would build a great temple
Before I formed thee in the belly I knew thee — I ordained thee a prophet unto the nations
He would be king over Israel
His name would be changed to Israel
verseText: Before I formed you in the belly I knew you; and before you cam forth out of the womb I sanctified you, and I ordained you a prophet to the nations.

question: In Ezekiel's vision of the dry bones, what happened after he prophesied to the bones?
choices: They were buried in a valley
They came together with sinews, flesh, and skin and then received breath and lived
They were swept into the sea
verseText: There was a noise, and behold a shaking, and the bones came together, bone to his bone... and breath came into them, and they lived.

question: Which prophet said 'They shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruninghooks'?
verseText: They shall beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruninghooks: nation shall not lift up sword against nation.

question: According to Zechariah, for how many pieces of silver was the good shepherd valued?
verseText: So they weighed for my price thirty pieces of silver... And the LORD said to me, Cast it to the potter.

question: Which prophet announced the 'Day of the Lord' and described locusts as an army?
verseText: Blow you the trumpet in Zion... for the day of the LORD comes.

question: Isaiah 9:6 names the child to be born with several titles. Which of these is NOT one of them?
verseText: His name shall be called Wonderful, Counsellor, The mighty God, The everlasting Father, The Prince of Peace.

question: What did Elijah's servant see on the seventh look over the Mediterranean that signaled rain was coming?
choices: A cloud as small as a man's hand
A rainbow stretching across the sky
A darkening of the whole sky
verseText: Behold, there ariseth a little cloud out of the sea, like a man's hand.

question: Malachi ends the Old Testament with a promise about which prophet returning before the Day of the Lord?
verseText: Behold, I will send you Elijah the prophet before the coming of the great and dreadful day of the LORD.

question: What did Ezekiel see that had four living creatures, four wheels within wheels, and the glory of God?
choices: A dream by the river Nile
A vision by the river Chebar
A trance in the temple court
A night vision on Mount Carmel
verseText: Now it came to pass... as I was among the captives by the river of Chebar... the heavens were opened, and I saw visions of God.

question: Which prophet said God desires mercy and not sacrifice, and the knowledge of God more than burnt offerings?
verseText: For I desired mercy, and not sacrifice; and the knowledge of God more than burnt offerings.

question: What did Daniel see in Nebuchadnezzar's dream that struck a statue of mixed materials?
verseText: You saw till that a stone was cut out without hands, which struck the image upon his feet.

question: Which prophet ran away to Tarshish instead of obeying God's call?
verseText: But Jonah rose up to flee to Tarshish from the presence of the LORD.

question: What did the angel touch in Isaiah's vision to cleanse the prophet's lips?
choices: A glowing coal from the altar
verseText: Then flew one of the seraphim... having a live coal in his hand... and he laid it upon my mouth, and said, Lo, this has touched your lips; and your iniquity is taken away.

question: Isaiah 40 begins with a message of comfort. What does God say about sins being pardoned?
choices: They are cast into the depths of the sea
Her iniquity is pardoned and she has received double for all her sins
They are forgiven and remembered no more
They are washed away like snow
verseText: Speak you comfortably to Jerusalem... that her iniquity is pardoned: for she has received of the LORD's hand double for all her sins.

question: According to Isaiah 7:14, what name would be given to the son born of the virgin, meaning 'God with us'?
verseText: Behold, a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.

question: Which prophet said God would restore Israel's fortunes and they would call on the Lord their God from captivity?
verseText: I will... gather her that was driven out... I will get them praise and fame in every land where they have been put to shame.

question: Elisha asked for what when Elijah was about to be taken from him?
choices: A double portion of Elijah's spirit
verseText: Elisha said, I pray you, let a double portion of your spirit be upon me.

question: In Ezekiel's vision, what did the four faces of the living creatures include?
verseText: As for the likeness of their faces, they four had the face of a man, and the face of a lion... the face of an ox... the face of an eagle.

question: What did Jeremiah buy as a symbolic act of hope while Jerusalem was under siege?
verseText: I bought the field... at Anathoth, and weighed him the money, even seventeen shekels of silver.

question: What did God show Amos in a vision involving a basket of ripe fruit?
choices: The end had come for Israel
verseText: Thus has the LORD God shewed to me: and behold a basket of summer fruit... The end is come upon my people of Israel.

question: Which prophet compared Israel's unfaithfulness to an adulterous wife whom God still loved and called back?
verseText: The Lord said to me, Go yet, love a woman beloved of her friend, yet an adulteress, according to the love of the LORD toward the children of Israel.

question: Which two prophets encouraged the returned exiles to rebuild the temple after the long delay?
verseText: Then the prophets, Haggai the prophet, and Zechariah the son of Iddo, prophesied to the Jews... in the name of the God of Israel.

question: Isaiah 53:4 says he bore our griefs and carried our sorrows. How did people misjudge him?
choices: They thought him a false prophet
They thought him stricken and smitten of God and afflicted
They thought him guilty of blasphemy
They thought him a rebel against Rome
verseText: Surely he has borne our griefs, and carried our sorrows: yet we did esteem him stricken, smitten of God, and afflicted.

question: Zechariah 12:10 says 'They shall look upon me whom they have pierced, and they shall mourn.' Who does the New Testament say this refers to?
verseText: And again another scripture says, They shall look on him whom they pierced.

question: What does Isaiah 40 compare the nations of the earth to in order to show God's greatness?
choices: A grain of sand on the seashore
A drop in a bucket and dust on the scales
A single blade of grass in a vast field
verseText: Behold, the nations are as a drop of a bucket, and are counted as the small dust of the balance.

question: Which prophet spoke of seventy weeks determined upon thy people?
verseText: Seventy weeks are determined for your people and for your holy city.

question: Which prophet described the coming Messenger who would prepare the way?
verseText: Behold, I send My messenger, and he will prepare the way before Me.

question: Which prophet said, 'They shall look upon me whom they have pierced'?
verseText: They will look on Me whom they pierced.

question: Which prophet foretold that Messiah would be cut off, but not for himself?
verseText: And after threescore and two weeks shall Messiah be cut off, but not for himself.

question: Which prophet was told to eat a scroll containing lamentations, mourning, and woe?
verseText: Son of man, eat that you find; eat this roll.

question: Choose One: The Bible says the Messiah would be born in Jerusalem.
verseText: But you, Bethlehem Ephratah... out of you shall he come forth to me.

question: Isaiah 53:5 says he was wounded for our transgressions and bruised for our iniquities. What does it say the chastisement brought?
verseText: The chastisement of our peace was upon him; and with his stripes we are healed.

question: Daniel 7 describes a vision of four beasts. What was the fourth beast like?
choices: A bear with ribs in its mouth
A terrible beast with iron teeth and ten horns
verseText: A fourth beast, dreadful and terrible, and strong exceedingly; and it had great iron teeth... and it had ten horns.

question: Which prophet wrote about the new covenant written on the heart in the context of both houses of Israel being restored?

question: In the parable of the mustard seed, the kingdom of heaven is likened to a seed that becomes what?
verseText: Which indeed is the least of all seeds: but when it is grown, it is the great among herbs, and becometh a tree.

question: In the parable of the workers in the vineyard, what did the laborers hired last receive?
verseText: They received every man a penny.

question: In the parable of the unforgiving servant, what enormous debt was forgiven?
verseText: One was brought to him, which owed him ten thousand talents.

question: In the parable of the dragnet, what happens after the net is full?
choices: Good are gathered and bad cast away
verseText: They gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away.

question: Who am I? I beat my breast and cried, 'God be merciful to me a sinner.'
verseText: The tax collector... struck upon his breast, saying, God be merciful to me a sinner.

question: In the parable of the workers in the vineyard, what time of day were the last workers hired?
verseText: And about the eleventh hour he went out, and found others standing idle.

question: What does the leaven hidden in three measures of meal represent in Jesus's parable?
choices: The kingdom of heaven spreading through all
The growth of faith over time
verseText: The kingdom of heaven is like leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.

question: In the parable of the unforgiving servant, what small debt did the forgiven servant refuse to cancel?
verseText: He found one of his fellowservants, which owed him an hundred pence.

question: In the parable of the two sons, one son said he would go work in the vineyard but didn't, and the other said he wouldn't but did. Which one did the father's will?
choices: The one who said no but went
verseText: He answered and said, I will not: but afterward he repented, and went.

question: What does the seed on thorny ground represent in Jesus's explanation of the parable of the sower?
choices: People who never hear the word
Those who hear but the cares of the world choke the word
Those who hear and bear fruit
People who follow the law strictly
verseText: These are they which are sown among thorns; such as hear the word, And the cares of this world... choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.

question: In the parable of the fig tree, what does it mean when the branches are tender and leaves come out?
verseText: When his branch is yet tender, and puts forth leaves, you know that summer is nigh.

question: In the parable of the prodigal son, why did the older brother refuse to come to the celebration?
choices: He was working in the field
He was angry that his brother was welcomed back with a feast despite wasting his inheritance
He was afraid his brother would ask for more money
He did not recognize his brother
verseText: He was angry, and would not go in... Lo, these many years do I serve you, neither transgressed I at any time your commandment.

question: In the parable of the Good Samaritan, who passed by the injured man without helping — first?
verseText: And by chance there came down a certain priest that way: and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.

question: In the parable of the rich man and Lazarus, where did Lazarus go after he died?
verseText: The beggar died, and was carried by the angels into Abraham's bosom.

question: In the parable of the rich man and Lazarus, what did the rich man see when he lifted his eyes in torment?
choices: Abraham afar off with Lazarus in his bosom
A great chasm he could not cross
verseText: He lift up his eyes, being in torments, and sees Abraham afar off, and Lazarus in his bosom.

question: In the parable of the ten virgins, what did the foolish virgins ask the wise virgins to share?
verseText: The foolish said to the wise, Give us of your oil; for our lamps are gone out.

question: In the parable of the unjust judge, why did the judge finally grant the widow's request?
choices: He was tired of her continual coming
verseText: Yet because this widow troubleth me, I will avenge her, l by her continual coming she weary me.

question: In the parable of the talents, what did the master say to the servant who hid his talent in the ground?
choices: You are forgiven — go in peace
Give it to the one with ten talents
verseText: His lord answered and said to him, You wicked and slothful servant.

question: In the parable of the prodigal son, the father said his son 'was dead, and is alive again.' What else did he say about him?
choices: He was lost and is found
He was blind and now sees
He was poor and is now rich
He was far off and is now near
verseText: For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found.

question: What does Jesus say is the meaning of the seed on the wayside in the parable of the sower?
choices: People who never hear the word
Those who hear but the devil takes away the word before it takes root
Those who hear but are too busy
Those who reject the word outright
verseText: Those by the way side are they that hear; then comes the devil, and takes away the word out of their hearts.

question: In the parable of the great banquet in Luke 14, what excuses did invited guests give for not coming?
choices: They were at war and could not leave
They had bought land, oxen, or married a wife
They were ill and could not travel
verseText: I have bought a piece of ground... I have bought five yoke of oxen... I have married a wife.

question: In the parable of the Good Samaritan, what did the Samaritan promise the innkeeper?
choices: He would send a reward on his return
That he would repay any extra expenses on his return
That the man would work off his debt
That the priests would pay the bill
verseText: Whatever you spend more, when I come again, I will repay you.

question: In the parable of the dragnet, what does the separation of fish represent?
choices: The division of Israel from the Gentiles
The separation of the wicked from the just at the end of the world
The sorting of clean from unclean animals under the law
The selection of disciples by Jesus
verseText: So shall it be at the end of the world: the angels shall come forth, and sever the wicked from among the just.

question: In the parable of the two sons asked to work in the vineyard, who does Jesus say will enter the kingdom before the chief priests and elders?
choices: Harlots and tax collectors who repented
verseText: Assuredly I say to you, That the tax collectors and the harlots go into the kingdom of God before you.

question: In the parable of the ten virgins, what was the bridegroom's response when the foolish virgins arrived late?
choices: He welcomed them but gave them no seat
Verily I say unto you, I know you not
You may enter but cannot eat
Wait until the feast is over
verseText: But he answered and said, Assuredly I say to you, I know you not.

question: Jesus said the kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed, but while he slept, an enemy did what?
verseText: But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way.

question: In the parable of the fig tree with no fruit, how long did the vineyard keeper ask the owner to wait before cutting it down?
verseText: Let it alone this year also, till I shall dig about it, and dung it.

question: In the parable of the rich man and Lazarus, what specific comfort did Lazarus receive in the afterlife that contrasted with his life?
choices: He was given a throne of honor
He was comforted, while the rich man who was comforted in life was now tormented
He was given rest from his suffering
He was seated at a great banquet
verseText: Son, remember that you in your lifetime receivedst your good things, and likewise Lazarus evil things: but now he is comforted, and you are tormented.

question: In the parable of the laborers in the vineyard, what did the owner say to the grumbling workers?
choices: You agreed to the wage — is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own?
Work harder and you will receive more tomorrow
Be grateful for any pay at all
The last shall be first and the first last
verseText: Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own? Is your eye evil, because I am good?

question: In the parable of the growing seed in Mark 4, what does the man do while the seed grows on its own?
choices: He sleeps and rises night and day while the earth brings forth fruit of herself
verseText: And should sleep, and rise night and day, and the seed should spring and grow up, he knows not how.

question: In the parable of the ten minas (pounds) in Luke 19, what happened to the servant whose mina was taken away?
choices: It was divided among the other servants equally
It was given to the one who had ten minas
It was put in the treasury
It was returned to the master
verseText: Take from him the pound, and give it to him that has ten pounds.

question: In the parable of the talents, what did the servant with one talent do with it?
verseText: But he that had received one went and digged in the earth, and hid his lord's money.

question: In the parable of the Pharisee and the publican, who went down to his house justified?
verseText: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified rather than the other.

question: In the parable of the wicked husbandmen, what did the owner finally send to the vineyard workers?
verseText: Having yet therefore one son, his wellbeloved, he sent him also last to them.

question: In the parable of the wedding feast, what was wrong with one of the guests?
choices: He sat in the wrong seat
verseText: He saw there a man which had not on a wedding garment.

question: In the parable of the rich fool, what was the rich man planning to build?
verseText: I will pull down my barns, and build greater.

question: In the parable of the pounds, what did the master say to the faithful servant?
choices: Take up thy bed and walk
verseText: Well, you good servant: because you have been faithful in a very little...

question: In the parable of the rich man and Lazarus, what was Abraham's reason for not sending Lazarus back to warn the rich man's brothers?
choices: Lazarus was too weak to travel
They have Moses and the prophets — let them hear them
It was against the laws of heaven
The brothers would not believe a dead man
verseText: Abraham says to him, They have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.

question: In the parable of the wicked husbandmen, what did they do to the owner's son when he arrived?
choices: They welcomed him and gave him the fruit
They cast him out of the vineyard and killed him
They sent him back to his father
verseText: They took him, and killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.

question: In the parable of the Pharisee and the tax collector, what did the Pharisee boast about in his prayer?
choices: His wealth given to the temple
His fasting twice a week and tithing of all he possessed
His many sacrifices at the altar
verseText: I fast twice in the week, I give tithes of all that I possess.

question: In the parable of the wedding feast, where did the king's servants go to find guests after the invited ones refused?
verseText: Go you therefore into the highways, and as many as you shall find, bid to the marriage.

question: In the parable of the unjust steward, why was the steward about to be dismissed?
choices: He was accused of stealing silver
He was accused of wasting his master's goods
He gave too much away to the poor
He lost a lawsuit for his master
verseText: There was a certain rich man, which had a steward; and the same was accused to him that he had wasted his goods.

question: In the parable of the pounds in Luke 19, how many pounds did each servant receive — unlike the varying talents of Matthew 25?
verseText: He called his ten servants, and delivered them ten pounds, and said to them, Occupy till I come.

question: What does Jesus say is the meaning of the tares among the wheat at the end of the age?
choices: The tares are hypocrites in the church, and the wheat are true believers
The Son of man's angels will gather the wicked out from among the just
The harvest represents the last day of Israel's history
The tares are Gentiles and the wheat are Jews
verseText: The Son of man shall send forth his angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that offend.

question: In the parable of the unforgiving servant, what punishment did the king give him after he refused to forgive his fellow servant?
choices: He was delivered to the tormentors till he paid all that was due
He was put in prison with no torment
verseText: His lord was furious, and delivered him to the tormentors, till he should pay all that was due to him.

question: Jesus said the parable of the tares and wheat should not be acted upon until harvest so that what would not happen?
choices: The wicked would not know they were being watched
The wheat would not be rooted up along with the tares
The angels would not be confused
The enemy would not know the plan
verseText: L while you gather up the tares, you root up also the wheat with them.

question: In the parable of the rich fool, God said to him, 'This night thy soul shall be required of thee.' What had the man said that very day?
choices: I will give half to the poor tomorrow
Soul, thou hast much goods laid up for many years; take thine ease
I will build a temple to honor God
My sons will inherit all that I have
verseText: I will say to my soul, Soul, you have much goods laid up for many years; take your ease, eat, drink, and be merry.

question: In the parable of the two debtors, one owed five hundred pence and one owed fifty. When both were forgiven, who did Jesus say would love the creditor more?
choices: The one who owed five hundred
Neither — the debt was too embarrassing
verseText: He to whom he forgave most. And he said to him, You have rightly judged.

question: What question prompted Jesus to tell the parable of the Good Samaritan?
choices: Who is the greatest in the kingdom?
How many times must I forgive?
What must I do to inherit eternal life?
verseText: But he, willing to justify himself, said to Jesus, And who is my neighbor?

question: In the parable of the talents, what did the master say would happen to anyone who has nothing?
choices: He shall be given another chance
Even what he has will be taken from him
He shall receive mercy from the king
He shall remain as he is
verseText: From him that has not shall be taken away even that which he has.

question: In the parable of the unjust steward, what surprising thing did Jesus commend about the steward?
choices: His shrewdness — the children of this world are wiser in their generation than children of light
His honesty in admitting his fault
verseText: The lord commended the unjust steward, because he had done wisely: for the children of this world are in their generation wiser than the children of light.

question: The parable of the wedding garment ends with a famous saying. What is it?
choices: Love your enemies and do good
Many are called, but few are chosen
Blessed are the pure in heart
verseText: For many are called, but few are chosen.

question: In the parable of the two debtors in Luke 7, what prompted Jesus to tell this parable?
choices: A question from the Pharisees about fasting
The Pharisee Simon's unspoken criticism of a sinful woman who anointed Jesus
A dispute about money between two disciples
A question about how God judges sin
verseText: When the Pharisee which had bidden him saw it, he spoke within himself... Jesus answering said to him, Simon, I have somewhat to say to you.

question: What parable did Nathan the prophet use to confront David about his sin with Bathsheba?
choices: A man with many sheep who stole a neighbor's one lamb
A vineyard owner whose workers refused to pay
A king who forgave a great debt
A shepherd who left ninety-nine sheep
verseText: There were two men in one city; the one rich, and the other poor... the rich man... took the poor man's lamb.

question: In the parable of the fig tree in Luke 13, for how many years had the owner been coming to look for fruit without finding any?
verseText: These three years I come seeking fruit on this fig tree, and find none.

question: In the parable of the great supper in Luke 14, what command did the host give after the first wave of replacement guests still left room?
choices: Go into the highways and hedges and compel them to come in
Send out more invitations to the wealthy
Reduce the feast to a simpler meal
verseText: Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel them to come in, that my house may be filled.

question: In the parable of the friend at midnight, what reason does Jesus say the man's friend finally gets up to help him?
choices: Because of the man's shameless persistence
Because the noise was waking his children
verseText: Though he will not rise and give him, because he is his friend, yet because of his importunity he will rise and give him as many as he needeth.

question: In the parable of the new wine and old wineskins, what happens if new wine is put into old bottles?
choices: The bottles break and the wine is spilled
verseText: Neither do men put new wine into old bottles: else the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish.

question: In the parable of the tares, who does Jesus say the enemy who sowed the tares represents?
choices: Those who fall away from faith
verseText: The enemy that sowed them is the devil; the harv is the end of the world.

question: In the parable of the good shepherd in John 10, how does the shepherd contrast with a hireling when the wolf comes?
choices: The shepherd calls for help; the hireling fights alone
The shepherd lays down his life for the sheep; the hireling flees
The shepherd protects the lambs only; the hireling protects all
The shepherd uses a staff; the hireling uses a sword
verseText: I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd gives his life for the sheep. But he that is an hireling... sees the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth.

question: What does Jesus say the field represents in his explanation of the parable of the tares?
verseText: The field is the world; the good seed are the children of the kingdom.

question: In the parable of the ten minas, what did the nobleman's citizens say before he departed?
choices: We will not have this man to reign over us
Give us a king from among us
We will wait for his return
verseText: But his citizens hated him, and sent a message after him, saying, We will not have this man to reign over us.

question: In the parable of the sheep and the goats in Matthew 25, on what basis are the sheep rewarded?
choices: They served Christ by feeding, welcoming, and visiting the least of his brethren
Their faithfulness in tithing and sacrifice
verseText: Inasmuch as you have done it to one of the least of these my brethren, you have done it to me.

question: In the parable of the vine and branches in John 15, what does Jesus say happens to branches that do not bear fruit?
choices: They are pruned lightly and given more time
They are taken away and cast into the fire
They are tied to the main vine for support
They are left to recover on their own
verseText: Every branch in me that beareth not fruit he takes away... he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and men gather them, and cast them into the fire.

question: In what city was Jesus born?
verseText: Now when Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judaea...

question: What river was Jesus baptized in?
verseText: Then Jesus came from Galilee to John at the Jordan to be baptized by him.

question: On what mountain did Noah's ark come to rest?
verseText: And the ark rested... upon the mountains of Ararat.

question: In what garden did Adam and Eve live?
verseText: The Lord God planted a garden eastward in Eden.

question: In what city was Jesus crucified?
verseText: And when they were come to the place which is called Calvary, there they crucified him.

question: What river flowed through Babylon where Judah's captives wept?
verseText: By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down, yea, we wept... among the captives by the river of Chebar.

question: Choose One: Paul was shipwrecked on the island of Patmos.
verseText: Paul was shipwrecked on Malta (Melita); John was exiled on Patmos.

question: In what country did Israel live as slaves before the Exodus?
verseText: They did set over them taskmasters to afflict them with their burdens. And they built for Pharaoh treasure cities.

question: In what town did Jesus grow up?
verseText: He went down with them, and came to Nazareth, and was subject to them.

question: What city was Paul from?
verseText: Paul said, I am a man which am a Jew of Tarsus, a city in Cilicia.

question: What garden did Jesus pray in the night he was arrested?
verseText: Then comes Jesus with them to a place called Gethsemane.

question: What sea did God part for Moses and the Israelites?
verseText: Moses stretched out his hand over the sea; and the Lord caused the sea to go back.

question: What body of water did Jesus walk on?
verseText: In the fourth watch of the night Jesus went to them, walking on the sea.

question: What was the name of the land God promised to Abraham and his descendants?
verseText: I will give to you, and to your seed after you, all the land of Canaan, for an everlasting possession.

question: What city did God tell Jonah to preach repentance to?
verseText: Arise, go to Nineveh, that great city, and cry out against it.

question: What mountain did Moses climb to receive the Ten Commandments?
verseText: And the Lord came down upon mount Sinai, on the top of the mount.

question: What city was King David's capital?
verseText: David took the strong hold of Zion: the same is the city of David.

question: What country did Joseph end up in after being sold by his brothers?
verseText: They drew and lifted up Joseph out of the pit... and brought Joseph into Egypt.

question: Where did Jesus ascend into heaven?
verseText: When he had spoken these things, while they beheld, he was taken up; and a cloud received him out of their sight.

question: What was the hometown of the disciples Peter, Andrew, and Philip?
verseText: Now Philip was of Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.

question: What city was home base for Jesus's ministry in Galilee?
verseText: Leaving Nazareth, he came and dwelt in Capernaum, which is upon the sea coast.

question: What city did the wise men first travel to when seeking the newborn Jesus?
verseText: There came wise men from the east to Jerusalem, saying, Where is he that is born King of the Jews?

question: In what country did Mary, Joseph, and baby Jesus flee to escape Herod?
verseText: Arise, and take the young child and his mother, and flee into Egypt.

question: Jesus performed his first miracle at a wedding in what town?
verseText: There was a marriage in Cana of Galilee; and the mother of Jesus was there.

question: The Israelites wandered in what wilderness for 40 years?
verseText: Your children shall wander in the wilderness forty years.

question: Where was Abraham living when God first called him?
verseText: The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham, when he was in Mesopotamia.

question: Where was the Last Supper held?
verseText: He will shew you a large upper room furnished and prepared: there make ready for us.

question: What was Ruth's homeland?
verseText: They took them wives of the women of Moab; the name of the one was Orpah, and the name of the other Ruth.

question: What city became the capital of the northern kingdom of Israel?
verseText: He called the name of the city which he built, after the name of Shemer, owner of the hill, Samaria.

question: Where was Solomon's temple built?
verseText: Solomon began to build the house of the LORD at Jerusalem.

question: What was the first city ever built, according to Genesis?
verseText: He builded a city, and called the name of the city, after the name of his son, Enoch.

question: Where did the wise men find the young child Jesus?
verseText: And when they were come into the house, they saw the young child with Mary his mother.

question: Where was Solomon's temple built?
verseText: Solomon began to build the house of the LORD at Jerusalem.

question: What mount overlooked Jerusalem and the temple from the east?
verseText: When they drew nigh to Jerusalem, and were come to Bethphage, to the mount of Olives.

question: From what town was Lazarus, whom Jesus raised from the dead?
verseText: Now a certain man was sick, named Lazarus, of Bethany...

question: What sea did Jesus often cross with his disciples during his ministry?
verseText: Let us pass over to the other side.

question: Whose city walls fell after Israel marched around them seven days?
verseText: And the wall fell down flat.

question: On what mountain did Elijah confront the prophets of Baal?
verseText: Gather to me... to mount Carmel.

question: To what city was Saul traveling when the heavenly light blinded him?
verseText: As he journeyed, he came near Damascus.

question: Where did Jonah try to flee when God sent him to Nineveh?
verseText: Jonah rose up to flee to Tarshish from the presence of the LORD.

question: What road was the traveler on when the Good Samaritan helped him?
verseText: A certain man went down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell among thieves.

question: What nation captured the northern kingdom of Israel in 722 BC?
verseText: The king of Assyria took Samaria, and carried Israel away into Assyria.

question: What nation carried the southern kingdom of Judah into captivity?
verseText: Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came, he, and all his host, against Jerusalem.

question: Where did Abraham offer Isaac as a sacrifice?
verseText: Get you into the land of Moriah; and offer him there for a burnt offering.

question: What city was home to the first Gentile church, where disciples were first called Christians?
verseText: The disciples were called Christians first in Antioch.

question: What is the name of the hill where Jesus was crucified?
verseText: He bearing his cross went forth into a place called the place of a skull, which is called in the Hebrew Golgotha.

question: Where did the two disciples encounter the risen Jesus on the road after his resurrection?
verseText: Two of them went that same day to a village called Emmaus... And Jesus himself drew near, and went with them.

question: What town was the birthplace of both King David and Jesus?
verseText: David was the son of that Ephrathite of Bethlehemjudah... Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judaea.

question: Where did Paul and Barnabas begin their first missionary journey?
verseText: The Holy Spirit said, Separate me Barnabas and Saul for the work whereunto I have called them.

question: In what region did Jesus do most of his ministry?
verseText: Jesus went about all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom.

question: Jesus passed through what region that Jews usually avoided because of ethnic tensions?
verseText: He left Judaea, and departed again into Galilee. And he must needs go through Samaria.

question: In what city did Paul spend 18 months establishing a church?
verseText: After these things Paul departed from Athens, and came to Corinth... And he continued there a year and six months.

question: Where did Elijah flee to after his victory over the prophets of Baal?
verseText: He went in the strength of that meat forty days and forty nights to Horeb the mount of God.

question: What river did Jacob wrestle with the angel beside?
verseText: He rose up that night, and passed over the ford Jabbok... and there wrestled a man with him until the breaking of the day.

question: Where did Jesus feed the 5,000?
choices: By the Sea of Galilee broadly
verseText: He took them, and went aside privately into a desert place belonging to the city called Bethsaida.

question: In what city did Paul and Silas experience an earthquake that freed them from jail?
verseText: At midnight Paul and Silas prayed, and sang praises to God... And suddenly there was a great earthquake.

question: What city did Paul visit on his third missionary journey, spending two years there?
verseText: This continued by the space of two years; so that all they which dwelt in Asia heard the word of the LORD Jesus.

question: Ruth and Naomi returned to what city after living in Moab?
verseText: So they two went until they came to Bethlehem.

question: What city did Nehemiah travel from Persia to rebuild its walls?
verseText: Send me to Judah, to the city of my fathers' sepulchres, that I may build it.

question: What region did the ten northern tribes form as their kingdom after splitting from Judah?
verseText: So Israel rebelled against the house of David... and made him king over all Israel.

question: What was the hometown of Ruth's mother-in-law Naomi?
verseText: A certain man of Bethlehemjudah went to sojourn in the country of Moab.

question: Paul preached on Mars Hill in which city?
verseText: Then Paul stood in the midst of Mars' hill, and said, You men of Athens.

question: Where did Abraham come from before settling in Canaan?
verseText: So Abram departed, as the Lord had spoken to him... and Abram was seventy and five years old when he departed out of Haran.

question: On what mount did Jesus deliver his famous sermon of beatitudes?
verseText: And seeing the multitudes, he went up into a mountain: and when he was set, his disciples came to him.

question: In what city were Daniel and his friends taken as captives?
verseText: Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came to Jerusalem, and besieged it.

question: What city did Zacchaeus the tax collector live in when Jesus visited him?
verseText: And Jesus entered and passed through Jericho.

question: What mountain top did Elijah stand on when he heard the still small voice of God?
verseText: And, behold, the Lord passed by... and after the earthquake a fire... and after the fire a still small voice.

question: Which island did Paul visit on his voyage to Rome after being shipwrecked?
verseText: And when they were escaped, then they knew that the island was called Melita.

question: Which road was Saul traveling on when he encountered the risen Christ?
verseText: As he journeyed, he came near Damascus: and suddenly there shined round about him a light from heaven.

question: On what mountain did Moses receive the Ten Commandments?
verseText: And the Lord came down upon mount Sinai... And he gave to Moses two tablets of the Testimony.

question: On what plain was the Tower of Babel built after the flood?
choices: Plain in the land of Shinar
verseText: They found a plain in the land of Shinar; and they dwelt there.

question: From what port did Paul depart when he sailed for Rome as a prisoner?
verseText: When it was determined that we should sail into Italy... Paul was delivered.

question: What brook did Elijah hide at, where ravens brought him food?
verseText: Hide thyself by the brook Cherith... and I have commanded the ravens to feed you there.

question: What mountain did Moses ascend to view the Promised Land before his death?
verseText: Moses went up from the plains of Moab to the mountain of Nebo... and the Lord shewed him all the land.

question: What island was the apostle John exiled to when he received his visions?
